name: roo_hotspot_chr2L_30B
description: Drosophila TE insertion hotspot: roo (a.k.a. B104) LTR-retrotransposon hotspot, ~100 copies in D. melanogaster. Drosophila genomes carry ~15-20% TE content. Insertion hotspots reflect both target-site preference and lower selection in low-recombination regions. Reads hitting these regions are typically misplaced unless full-length insertion-specific anchors are available.
expected_copy_count: 10-100 copies of the named TE family genome-wide; locally enriched 3-10x baseline
